originalTitle: Dozens killed as Angola flood death toll rises
title: Big Rains and Floods in Angola: People Helping Each Other
content:
Hello, Friends! From Your African News Reporter!

Hi there, wonderful friends! It's your friendly African news reporter here, bringing you important news from across our amazing continent. Today, we're going to talk about something that happened in a beautiful country called Angola. Angola is a very special place in Africa, and recently, they experienced some very, very heavy rain. So much rain fell that it caused big floods, which means too much water covering the land. When the water gets too high, it can be very sad. Sadly, many people were affected, and some even lost their lives. The grown-ups who lead the country, like the President, are very sad about this. They are working super hard to help everyone. We want to understand what happened and how people are being brave and kind to each other.

What Exactly is a Flood?

Imagine you are watering your favourite plant, and you accidentally spill a huge bucket of water on the floor. The water spreads out everywhere, right? Well, a flood is just like that, but on a much, much bigger scale! When it rains for many, many days without stopping, the rivers get too full. They can't hold all that water anymore! So, the water overflows from the riverbanks and spreads out onto the land where people live, work, and play. It covers roads, goes into houses, and fills up fields. This is what we call a flood. It can be very scary and make it hard for people to move around safely. It's like the land becomes a giant lake! **Did you know?** Water can look calm, but a strong flood current can be very powerful, like a giant invisible hand pushing things!

Where in Africa is Angola?

Angola is a big and beautiful country located on the western side of Africa. If you look at a map of Africa, you'll see it right there, next to the big blue Atlantic Ocean. It shares its borders with other African countries like Namibia, Zambia, and Congo. Angola is known for its lovely beaches, big forests, and the friendly smiles of its people. Just like many places in Africa, Angola has different seasons. Sometimes, it gets a lot of rain, especially during what they call the "rainy season." This rain is usually good because it helps plants grow and keeps the land green. But sometimes, too much rain falls all at once, more than the ground or rivers can handle, and that's when floods can happen. **Did you know?** The capital city of Angola is Luanda, and it's a very busy and exciting place!

What Happens When Too Much Water Comes?

When a flood happens, it can cause a lot of problems for people. Imagine waking up and finding water inside your home! Your beds, toys, and furniture can get wet and spoiled. People often have to leave their homes very quickly to find a safe place to stay, sometimes with neighbours or in big shelters. It's very upsetting to leave your home behind. Roads also get covered with water, making it impossible for cars, buses, or even bicycles to pass. This means children can't go to school, and grown-ups can't go to work or the market to buy food. The water can even be strong enough to knock down small buildings or fences. It truly turns everything upside down for a while. **Did you know?** Floodwaters can sometimes carry dirt, rubbish, and even small animals, so it's very important to stay away from them!

Brave People Helping Each Other

Even when things are really difficult and sad, the most wonderful thing happens: people come together to help each other. In Angola, there are many brave people, like special rescuers, who are working tirelessly. They are like real-life superheroes, using boats to reach people who are stuck in their homes because of the water. These kind rescuers help everyone, especially children and older people, get to safety. But it's not just the rescuers! Neighbours are helping neighbours, sharing their food, giving out warm blankets, and offering a safe place to sleep to those who lost their homes. This is what makes Africa so special – our strong sense of community and care for one another. **Did you know?** Helping someone in need can make you feel happy too, because kindness is a powerful thing!

The President's Sadness and Hope for Angola

The leader of Angola is called the President. He is like the head of a very big family, which is the whole country. When these floods happened, the President felt very, very sad for all the people who got hurt or lost loved ones. He said that finding everyone affected was like a "race against the clock." This means they had to work incredibly fast, every single minute counting, to find people who might still need help or be stuck. The President and his team are doing everything they can to support the families who are mourning, which means being very sad about losing someone. They are also planning how to rebuild all the damaged homes and fix the roads, so everyone can get back to their normal lives soon. **Did you know?** A president has a very important job, making sure everyone in their country is safe and cared for.

Staying Safe from Big Waters

It's really good to learn about things like floods, so we know how to keep ourselves safe if we ever see a lot of water. If you live in an area where it rains a lot, always listen very carefully to what your parents and other grown-ups tell you. They will have important instructions to keep you out of harm's way. Never, ever try to play in floodwater, even if it looks like fun. It can be much deeper than you think, and the water can be moving very fast. There might also be dangerous things hidden under the water that you cannot see, like sharp objects or even electrical wires. Always stay with a grown-up and obey their safety rules when there's a lot of water around. **Did you know?** Even a small amount of moving water can be strong enough to sweep a person off their feet!

Remembering Our Friends and Looking Forward

It's so important that we remember all the people who were lost in these floods. They were someone's child, parent, or friend. We send our kindest thoughts and biggest hugs to their families in Angola. Even though we are not there, we can think about them and hope they feel better soon. The people of Angola are very strong and brave. They will work together, hand-in-hand, to clean up all the mess and rebuild their homes and villages. It will take time, hard work, and lots of help, but they will surely make their country beautiful and strong again. We hope the sun shines brightly on Angola very, very soon, bringing warmth and happiness back to everyone. **Did you know?** When people help each other, even big problems can become a little bit easier to solve! Keep learning, keep shining, and always remember to be kind to everyone around you. This is your African news reporter, signing off with a message of hope and strength for our friends in Angola. Bye for now!
summary: Heavy rains caused big floods in Angola, leading to sadness and loss, but brave people and the President are working hard together to help everyone and rebuild their homes and lives.
whyMatters: This is important because floods show us how strong nature can be, and it teaches us that even when bad things happen, people in Africa always come together to help each other and show great kindness and strength.

originalTitle: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
title: Our African Football Friends Are on a New Journey!
content:
Hello, Future Heroes!

Hi everyone! Your friendly African news reporter is here! Today, we have an interesting story about some super football players from a beautiful country called Eritrea. It's a bit like a mystery, but we'll try to understand it together! Football is such a popular game, isn't it? Lots of running, kicking, and cheering! It brings people together from all over the world. Our story is about seven young men who love playing football.

A Football Game Far Away!

These seven footballers are part of the national team from Eritrea. This means they are the very best players chosen to represent their country! Imagine how proud they must feel! They traveled to another country to play a very important international match. An 'international match' means a game between two different countries. It's a big deal! After playing their hearts out, it was time for the team to pack their bags and fly back home to Eritrea. Everyone was ready to go.

Meet Our Eritrean Team!

Eritrea is a lovely country in Africa, right next to the Red Sea. It has beautiful beaches and old, interesting cities. Many people in Eritrea love football, just like you might! Our team worked very hard to train for their games. They practiced passing, shooting, and scoring goals. Playing for your country is a big honor! Did you know? Eritrea's capital city is called Asmara. It's famous for its unique old buildings!

Oops! Not Everyone Came Back!

Here's the tricky part of our story. When the time came for the team to travel back home, seven of these football players did not get on the plane. They decided to stay in the country where they played the game. It means they didn't go back to Eritrea with their teammates and coaches. They chose a different path. This might sound a bit puzzling, right? Why would someone not go home with their team after a match?

Why Didn't They Go Home?

Well, sometimes people dream of new adventures. They might want to explore new places or try living somewhere different. Our footballers are young men with big dreams. Maybe they wanted to find new opportunities. Sometimes, people hope to find a place where they feel very safe and happy, and where they can follow their dreams more easily. Did you know? Football players often travel all around the world for games. It's a great way to see new places! These players might have felt that staying in this new country would give them a chance for a different kind of life or a better future for their football careers. It's like when you dream of being a pilot or a doctor; sometimes you need to go to a special school or a special place to make that dream come true.

This Has Happened Before!

This isn't the first time that Eritrean athletes, like footballers, have decided not to go back home after an international trip. In the past, other players from Eritrea have also chosen to stay in different countries after playing matches or competing in sports events. It tells us that perhaps some people from Eritrea are looking for new beginnings and chances in other parts of the world. Did you know? The most popular sport in Africa is football! Almost everyone loves to watch or play it.

What Do Footballers Dream Of?

Every footballer dreams of playing well, scoring goals, and maybe even becoming famous! They want to be the best they can be. They also dream of a life where they can practice their sport freely and have many chances to play in big leagues. For these seven players, it seems they are hoping that staying in a new country will help them achieve those dreams. It's all about finding a place where you can grow, learn, and be happy doing what you love.

Everyone Wants to Be Happy and Safe!

No matter where you live, everyone wants to feel safe and happy. They want to be able to make choices about their future and follow their hearts. These players are looking for a place where they feel they can do that. It's a very human thing to want to be secure and content. Did you know? There are 11 players on a football team on the field at one time! Plus, many more who help them train and play. We hope that wherever these seven footballers are, they are safe and happy, and that they get to continue playing the game they love so much.

What Happens Next?

We don't know exactly what will happen to them next. They might find new teams to play for, or they might start a completely different life. It's a big decision they made, and we wish them all the best on their new journey.

Let's Send Them Good Wishes!

So, remember our Eritrean football friends! They are on an adventure to find their own happy path. Let's send them good thoughts and hope they find all the joy and success they are looking for. Keep loving football, keep dreaming big, and keep exploring the wonderful world around you! That's all for today from your African news friend! See you next time!
summary: Seven football players from Eritrea decided not to go home with their team after a big game, choosing to start new adventures in a new country instead.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone, even football players, wants to feel safe and happy and follow their dreams, no matter where they are from.
